---
title: "Accelerometer Learning Course"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.course-home}

::: {.site-hero}
::: {.site-hero__shade}
:::

::: {.site-hero__inner}
<div class="brand-lockup">
<img src="images/lapaista-logo.png" alt="LA PAISTA laboratory logo" />
<span>University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign / LA PAISTA Lab</span>
</div>

<p class="eyebrow">Accelerometer Learning Course</p>
<h1>Turn raw movement signals into reproducible evidence.</h1>
<p class="hero-lead">A practical online course for planning accelerometer studies, processing raw movement files, checking quality, and building clean analysis-ready datasets.</p>
<div class="hero-actions">
<a class="button button-primary" href="accelerometer-introduction.html">Start Module 1</a>
<a class="button button-secondary" href="#course-modules">View modules</a>
</div>
:::
:::

::: {.metrics-band aria-label="Course summary"}
<div class="metric"><strong>8</strong><span>applied modules</span></div>
<div class="metric"><strong>3</strong><span>core tools: ActiLife, GGIR, Stata</span></div>
<div class="metric"><strong>1</strong><span>end-to-end reproducible workflow</span></div>
:::

::: {.section-wrap .intro-split}
::: {.section-copy}
<p class="section-kicker">Course overview</p>
<h2>Built for research teams who need clean accelerometer data.</h2>
<p>Accelerometry turns continuous movement signals into information about activity, sedentary behavior, and sleep. This course follows the practical decisions that make that transformation credible: device setup, file organization, processing, quality control, cleaning, and documentation.</p>
<p>The examples use ActiGraph, GGIR in R, and Stata. The principles are broader than any one device or software package, and every study should document its own protocol and analytical decisions.</p>
:::

<figure class="media-feature">
<video class="course-video" controls preload="none" poster="images/course-hero.png">
<source src="media/welcome-video.mp4" type="video/mp4" />
Your browser does not support the video tag. You can download the welcome video from <a href="media/welcome-video.mp4">this link</a>.
</video>
<figcaption>Welcome video for the Accelerometer Learning Course.</figcaption>
</figure>
:::

::: {.workflow-section #workflow}
::: {.section-wrap}
<p class="section-kicker">Workflow</p>
<h2>From device deployment to analysis-ready evidence.</h2>

::: {.workflow-grid}
<div class="workflow-step"><span>01</span><strong>Collect</strong><p>Initialize devices, define placement and wear protocol, and support participant compliance.</p></div>
<div class="workflow-step"><span>02</span><strong>Download</strong><p>Retrieve raw recordings and document return status, dates, and operator decisions.</p></div>
<div class="workflow-step"><span>03</span><strong>Organize</strong><p>Use reproducible folders, IDs, file naming, conversion logs, and transparent storage.</p></div>
<div class="workflow-step"><span>04</span><strong>Process</strong><p>Run GGIR or another approved workflow to calibrate, detect non-wear, and summarize movement.</p></div>
<div class="workflow-step"><span>05</span><strong>Inspect</strong><p>Review plots, missingness, calibration, clipping, valid days, and unusual movement patterns.</p></div>
<div class="workflow-step"><span>06</span><strong>Deliver</strong><p>Clean, standardize, derive outcomes, prepare Stata datasets, and report analytical decisions.</p></div>
:::
:::
:::

::: {.section-wrap #course-modules}
::: {.section-heading-row}
<div>
<p class="section-kicker">Course map</p>
<h2>Modules designed around the real research workflow.</h2>
</div>
<a class="text-link" href="accelerometer-introduction.html">Begin with Module 1</a>
:::

<div class="module-grid">
<div class="module-card module-card-active"><a href="accelerometer-introduction.html"><span>Module 1</span><strong>Accelerometer Introduction</strong><span class="card-text">What accelerometers record, what they can estimate, and how placement and protocol shape interpretation.</span></a></div>
<div class="module-card"><a href="accelerometer-programming-and-downloading.html"><span>Module 2</span><strong>Programming and Downloading</strong><span class="card-text">Configure monitors, download recordings, and assess whether collection was complete.</span></a></div>
<div class="module-card"><a href="organizing-and-converting.html"><span>Module 3</span><strong>Organizing and Converting</strong><span class="card-text">Create reproducible folders, file names, batches, and conversion logs.</span></a></div>
<div class="module-card"><a href="setting-up-r-and-ggir.html"><span>Module 4</span><strong>Setting Up R and GGIR</strong><span class="card-text">Prepare R/RStudio, define inputs and outputs, and understand the GGIR workflow.</span></a></div>
<div class="module-card"><a href="checking-data-quality.html"><span>Module 5</span><strong>Checking Data Quality</strong><span class="card-text">Review summaries and visual patterns; document concerns and decisions.</span></a></div>
<div class="module-card"><a href="cleaning-and-standardizing.html"><span>Module 6</span><strong>Cleaning and Standardizing</strong><span class="card-text">Clean GGIR outputs, standardize variables, and preserve a transparent data trail.</span></a></div>
<div class="module-card"><a href="setting-up-final-dataset-in-stata.html"><span>Module 7</span><strong>Final Dataset in Stata</strong><span class="card-text">Import, append, verify, and prepare the final analysis dataset.</span></a></div>
<div class="module-card"><a href="knowledge-checking.html"><span>Module 8</span><strong>Knowledge Checking</strong><span class="card-text">Consolidate the workflow and test key decisions before using study data.</span></a></div>
</div>
:::

<div class="section-wrap resource-strip">
<div class="resource-card"><a href="glossary.html"><span>Study aid</span><strong>Glossary</strong><span class="card-text">Key accelerometer, GGIR, QC, and Stata terms used across the course.</span></a></div>
<div class="resource-card"><a href="references.html"><span>Evidence base</span><strong>References</strong><span class="card-text">Bibliographic sources and software documentation cited in the modules.</span></a></div>
</div>

::: {.section-wrap .decision-panel}
<div>
<p class="section-kicker">Protocol standard</p>
<h2>Every parameter needs a study reason.</h2>
</div>
<p>Device configuration, valid-day rules, wear-time criteria, thresholds, and processing arguments must be selected for the study population, device, wear location, and protocol. This course teaches the decision process; it does not prescribe one universal parameter set.</p>
:::

:::
